question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted configuration entry into the `grpc` map with a forged scenario name so that `grpc.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` assigns an attacker-controlled name to a subscriber property, causing downstream transaction authorization to proceed under a spoofed identity without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `rest` map entry whose key passes the `StringUtils.isBlank` check but contains whitespace-only padding, bypassing the blank-name guard in `validate()` and causing `rest.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` to register a near-blank scenario name that authorizes transactions under an unverified identity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `validate()` only checks `Sets.intersection(grpc.keySet(), rest.keySet())` for duplicate names but does not verify ownership of those scenario names, allowing them to register a scenario name identical to a legitimate owner's scenario and authorize transactions under that owner's identity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `enabled = true` and inject a single minimal `grpc` or `rest` entry to pass the `if (enabled && grpc.isEmpty() && rest.isEmpty())` guard in `validate()`, thereby activating the subscription pipeline and triggering transaction monitoring flows that authorize transactions without the original signer's consent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user provide a `grpc` map and a `rest` map with zero overlapping keys to pass the `Sets.intersection` check in `validate()`, while both maps reference the same underlying transaction topic, causing dual-path subscription that authorizes the same transaction twice without the signer's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user craft a scenario name that is non-blank but semantically equivalent to an existing owner-controlled scenario name (e.g., Unicode normalization bypass), passing `StringUtils.isBlank` in `validate()` while colliding with a legitimate scenario at the transaction authorization layer, causing unauthorized transaction approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `enabled = false` to bypass the `if (enabled && grpc.isEmpty() && rest.isEmpty())` check in `validate()`, then re-enable the service at runtime without re-running `validate()`, causing subscription scenarios to activate without the authorization checks that `validate()` enforces?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a `grpc` entry with a `topicId` pointing to a topic owned by another account, and since `validate()` performs no ownership verification of `topicId`, cause the monitor to subscribe to and relay transactions from that topic without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `rest` map with a `publishers` set containing a legitimate publisher's scenario name, and since `validate()` does not verify publisher ownership, cause the REST subscriber to intercept and process transactions published by that legitimate publisher without their authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `clients` to the maximum value of 1024 in `SubscribeProperties`, passing the `@Max(1024)` constraint, to spawn 1024 subscriber clients that each independently attempt to authorize or relay transactions, overwhelming the signer's ability to detect or reject unauthorized transaction flows?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `statusFrequency` to the minimum allowed `Duration.ofSeconds(1)` in `SubscribeProperties`, causing the monitor to emit status updates at maximum frequency and masking unauthorized transaction authorization events within a flood of legitimate status noise?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user register a scenario name in the `grpc` map that exactly matches a name used by a REST scenario in a different deployment, and since `validate()` only checks intersection within the same `SubscribeProperties` instance, cause cross-instance transaction authorization confusion where transactions are approved under the wrong scenario's identity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@PostConstruct` lifecycle of `validate()` to inject a malicious `grpc` or `rest` entry before the Spring context fully initializes, causing the subscription pipeline to start with attacker-controlled parameters that authorize transactions without owner sign-off?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user provide a `grpc` map entry where the key is a valid non-blank string but the associated `GrpcSubscriberProperties` has a `topicId` set to a topic controlled by a high-value account, and since `validate()` does not check topic ownership, cause the monitor to authorize relaying of that account's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a `rest` map entry with `samplePercent = 1.0` and an empty `publishers` set, causing `shouldSample()` in `RestSubscriber` to return true for every published transaction regardless of origin, effectively authorizing the monitor to process all transactions without any publisher's explicit consent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `subscribers` to the maximum 1024 in `AbstractSubscriberProperties` for a `rest` entry, causing `createSubscriptions()` in `RestSubscriber` to spawn 1024 parallel REST subscriptions that each independently query and process transactions, amplifying unauthorized transaction authorization attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `subscribers` to 1024 in a `grpc` entry's `AbstractSubscriberProperties`, causing `createSubscriptions()` in `GrpcSubscriber` to create 1024 gRPC subscriptions to the same topic, each independently receiving and potentially acting on transaction messages without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `grpc` map with a `startTime` set to `Instant.EPOCH` in `GrpcSubscriberProperties`, causing the gRPC subscriber to replay all historical transactions from the beginning of the ledger, authorizing processing of transactions that were originally signed and submitted by other owners without their current consent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `startTime` to a far-future `Instant` in `GrpcSubscriberProperties`, causing `getEndTime()` to return a time far in the future, keeping the gRPC subscription open indefinitely and continuously authorizing transaction relay without the topic owner's ongoing approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a `rest` map entry with `timeout` set to the minimum `Duration.ofMillis(500)`, causing `clientSubscribe()` in `RestSubscriber` to aggressively retry timed-out transaction lookups via `retryWhen(retrySpec)`, effectively authorizing repeated unauthorized transaction queries against the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `limit` to 0 in `RestSubscriberProperties`, causing `getLimit()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, making the REST subscription process an unlimited number of transactions without any cap, authorizing unbounded transaction relay without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user set `maxAttempts` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` in the retry configuration of a `grpc` entry (as set by default in `GrpcSubscriberProperties` constructor), causing the gRPC subscriber to retry indefinitely on failures and continuously attempt to authorize transaction subscriptions without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `validate()` calls `grpc.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` without verifying that the name matches any registered signer or owner, allowing the attacker to assign arbitrary names to subscriber properties and impersonate legitimate transaction signers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `validate()` calls `rest.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` without any cryptographic verification of the name-to-owner binding, allowing the attacker to register a REST subscriber under a name that mimics a legitimate transaction signer and authorize transactions on their behalf?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged user provide a `grpc` map with a key that is a valid non-blank string but contains special characters (e.g., path traversal sequences), bypassing `StringUtils.isBlank` in `validate()` while causing downstream systems that use the scenario name to authorize transactions to misinterpret the name and grant unauthorized access?
